title: Alchemilla podophylla
intro: Alchemilla podophylla, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and rounded, lobed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and menstrual problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Alchemilla podophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Alchemilla podophylla is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are commonly used to soothe digestive issues, alleviate menstrual cramps, and promote wound healing.
